headline: 5th annual Glass City JazzFest announces August 14–15 lineup at Toledo Zoo Amphitheater and Glass City Metropark
paragraphs: The 5th annual Glass City JazzFest announced its 2026 lineup on July 1. The two-day festival returns August 14–15: the evening of August 14 at the Toledo Zoo Amphitheater (6–10:30 p.m.; $10 advance, $20 advance reserved, $20 day-of general admission), followed by a free all-day session August 15 at Glass City Metropark (1–10:30 p.m.), the signature riverside venue on the Glass City Riverwalk corridor. TARTA operates transit service to the event.

The dual-venue format places a ticketed cultural draw at the Toledo Zoo and a zero-barrier crowd day at Glass City Metropark on the same mid-August weekend. The Metropark's free day is the economic activation element: it brings thousands of visitors to the riverfront without a ticket price threshold, generating foot traffic for the Maumee riverside restaurants and vendors who rely on high-visibility summer Saturdays to anchor their revenue base.
why_it_matters: Now in its 5th year, Glass City JazzFest is one of the Metropark system's most established mid-summer events and a direct test of the 'completed Riverwalk section as event attractor' thesis. Free attendance at Glass City Metropark on August 15 creates a no-barrier crowd day for the riverfront that supports the foot-traffic arguments Toledo EDOs and mixed-use developers near the corridor use in their investment cases. The dual-venue model — pairing a ticketed evening at the Zoo Amphitheater with a free riverfront day — also demonstrates the Metroparks-Toledo Zoo programming partnership maturing as a regional event-calendar anchor.
